overview: Fix two post-integration bugs: (1) CDN URLs have a double slash due to a trailing slash on scene7_domain, and (2) asset thumbnails are not visible because the Data/royal directory is not served as static files by Express.
goals: Produce correct CDN URLs without double slashes
Serve asset thumbnails and web images via Express so the frontend can display them
nonGoals: No changes to the database schema
No changes to the ingest logic
successMetrics: GET /api/assets/:id returns cdn_url with exactly one slash after the domain (no double slash)
GET /api/assets/media/<thumbnail_path> returns the image file with 200
Asset cards in the React UI display thumbnails instead of broken images

routes.name: Serve DAM media files
routes.purpose: Static file serving for thumbnail and web image renditions stored in DATA_DIR

uiNotes: AssetCard thumbnail src should be constructed as: http://localhost:3001/api/assets/media/<thumbnail_path>
If thumbnail_path is null, keep the existing placeholder/fallback
rules: cdn_url construction: strip trailing slash from scene7_domain before concatenating — `scene7_domain.replace(/\/+$/, '') + '/is/image/' + scene7_file`
Media route: resolve DATA_DIR relative to server/src/index.js (i.e. path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', process.env.DATA_DIR || '../Data/royal'))
Media route must be registered BEFORE the catch-all 404 handler
Media route should use express.static() with the resolved DATA_DIR as root
Frontend: AssetCard builds thumbnail URL as `${API_BASE}/api/assets/media/${asset.thumbnail_path}` only when thumbnail_path is non-null

stories.title: Fix CDN URL double slash in assets route
stories.description: As a content author, I want the CDN URL in the asset detail to be a valid URL so I can copy and use it directly.
acceptanceCriteria: In server/src/routes/assets.js, update cdn_url construction to strip trailing slash from scene7_domain: `row.scene7_domain.replace(/\/+$/, '') + '/is/image/' + row.scene7_file`

GET /api/assets/dubai-arabian-gulf-emirates-burj-al-arab-skyline.jpg returns cdn_url equal to 'https://assets.dm.rccl.com/is/image/RoyalCaribbeanCruises/dubai-arabian-gulf-emirates-burj-al-arab-skyline' (single slash after domain)
Supertest test: cdn_url for any asset with scene7_domain set does not contain '//'
Supertest test: cdn_url for an asset with no scene7_file is null
Negative case: if scene7_domain has multiple trailing slashes (e.g. 'https://assets.dm.rccl.com///'), cdn_url still has exactly one slash before 'is/image'

stories.title: Serve DAM media files via Express static route
stories.description: As a content author, I want to see asset thumbnails in the browse grid so I can visually identify assets.
acceptanceCriteria: In server/src/index.js, add a static file route: app.use('/api/assets/media', express.static(path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', process.env.DATA_DIR || '../Data/royal')))
Import path from 'path' at the top of index.js if not already imported
Route is registered before any error-handling or 404 middleware

Supertest test: GET /api/assets/media/ships/allure/allure-of-the-seas-aerial-sailing-blue-sky.JPG/_jcr_content/renditions/cq5dam.thumbnail.319.319.png returns 200 or 404 (not 500) — verifies route is wired up

stories.title: Update AssetCard to load thumbnails from media route
stories.description: As a content author, I want asset cards in the browse grid to display the actual thumbnail image so I can visually identify assets.
acceptanceCriteria: In client/src/components/AssetCard.jsx (or wherever the thumbnail img src is set), update src to: asset.thumbnail_path ? `http://localhost:3001/api/assets/media/${asset.thumbnail_path}` : null
If thumbnail_path is null, render a placeholder div (grey background with filename text) — do not render a broken
Vitest test: AssetCard with thumbnail_path renders an with src containing '/api/assets/media/'
Vitest test: AssetCard with thumbnail_path=null renders a placeholder element, not an
Negative case: if the media URL returns a 404 (image not found on disk), the img onError handler swaps to the placeholder — no broken image icon shown
